messages.subject: R-beta: Plots with NT
messages.body_plain:
Jeff (and other interested parties),

  Once the size of the window has been changed there is no easy way to
  redraw the current plot and have it look nice. So the behaviour is to
  force you to redraw on resize. 
  
  Currently I am using bitmaps for the on-screen drawing and could stretch
  them to fit but the result is pretty horrific. I could use metafiles 
  (enhanced ones on NT) and get a better result but it still would not
  be very good. Splus redraws on resize and in my opinion it isn't very
  nice in most cases.
  
  However if there is a strong desire for this behaviour it can be done.
  
  robert

messages.subject: R-beta: Plots with NT
messages.body_plain:
From: Jeff Morris <morris at kodak.com>

	I've downloaded the latest Windows version of R and it looks great,
	but when I change the size of the graphics window, the plot disappears.
	Is this typical behavior?  I had thought that the Restore command
	in the graphics window would redraw but it is not highlighted.  Is
	there a quick way to restore the plot.  I working with NT 4.0

I'm not sure about the specifics of the windows version(s), but this
is a general problem in R graphics.  We are working on a solution
(which will also include multiple device drivers and copying between
devices).  The code should be ready to roll later in the year.

It hasn't seemed worthwhile producing solutions for specific devices
when such a general solution is in the works.
	Ross
